positions.claim: AI inference workloads will drive a structural memory supercycle lasting through at least 2027, fundamentally reshaping DRAM and NAND demand patterns beyond traditional computing cycles
implicitAssumptions: AI model architectures will continue scaling context windows and agent complexity rather than optimizing for memory efficiency
Memory production capacity expansion will lag demand growth sufficiently to maintain tight supply-demand conditions through 2026-2027
Hyperscalers will prioritize performance and capability over cost optimization in their infrastructure buildout decisions during this period

positions.claim: KV Cache storage represents a fundamental architectural shift that transforms NAND from passive storage into active computational infrastructure, creating 75-100 EB of new demand by 2027
implicitAssumptions: The power consumption cost of prefill computation exceeds the cost of persistent KV Cache storage at scale
Enterprise SSDs cannot be upgraded fast enough to meet bandwidth requirements, necessitating new High-Bandwidth Flash technology adoption
Data reuse patterns in LLM/RAG systems will intensify rather than diminish as models mature

positions.claim: Agentic AI workloads create qualitatively different infrastructure demands than training or simple inference, requiring concurrent low-latency memory access and driving CPU demand growth alongside accelerators
implicitAssumptions: Multi-agent systems will become the dominant AI deployment pattern rather than remaining niche applications
Iterative reasoning loops and elongated context windows cannot be efficiently collapsed through algorithmic optimization
CPU manufacturers have sufficient pricing power to benefit from tight supply conditions despite agent workloads being less compute-intensive than training

positions.claim: The memory industry is transitioning from volatile quarterly negotiations to long-term agreements (LTAs) with fixed volumes and prices, fundamentally reducing cyclicality and improving earnings visibility
implicitAssumptions: Hyperscaler customers prefer supply security over spot market optionality even when it means locking in higher prices
Memory suppliers have sufficient market power to enforce LTA terms rather than being forced back to quarterly negotiations during any demand softness
The structural nature of AI demand makes long-term capacity planning more reliable than in previous computing cycles

positions.claim: Storage I/O bandwidth is becoming the primary bottleneck in AI infrastructure, more constraining than compute capacity, necessitating architectural innovations like High-Bandwidth Flash
implicitAssumptions: AI workload characteristics are fundamentally memory-bound rather than compute-bound at current and projected scales
Existing enterprise SSD technology cannot be incrementally improved to meet bandwidth requirements within relevant timeframes
The economics of AI inference favor storage-intensive architectures over recomputation strategies

positions.claim: Chinese AI labs have achieved technological parity with U.S. counterparts in frontier model capabilities, reducing dependence on foreign APIs and validating domestic semiconductor ecosystem development
implicitAssumptions: Benchmark performance and specific technical achievements (like GLM 5's ranking) accurately reflect production-ready capability rather than optimized demo performance
Chinese companies can maintain this parity despite ongoing semiconductor export restrictions
Domestic model capability directly translates to reduced foreign API consumption rather than markets remaining segmented by other factors

positions.claim: Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) rather than raw chip performance is becoming the decisive competitive factor in AI compute, enabling AMD and Google TPU to challenge NVIDIA's dominance
implicitAssumptions: AI workloads have matured sufficiently that performance differences between competing platforms are within acceptable tolerance ranges for cost-sensitive buyers
Software ecosystem lock-in effects favoring NVIDIA (CUDA, libraries) can be overcome by sufficient TCO advantages
Meta's willingness to test TPUs signals broader industry willingness to diversify rather than being an isolated case

positions.claim: Advanced packaging capacity (CoWoS-L, RDL interposers) represents a more critical bottleneck than chip fabrication, driving strategic outsourcing decisions and capacity reallocation
implicitAssumptions: Packaging complexity scales faster than fabrication complexity for AI accelerators in current and near-future generations
The margin structure of advanced packaging justifies keeping complex steps in-house while outsourcing simpler substrate work
Packaging capacity cannot be expanded as quickly as fab capacity due to technical or capital constraints

positions.claim: 2025 AI PCs and edge devices are primarily marketing-driven rather than delivering genuine on-device inference capability due to memory bandwidth and model compression bottlenecks
implicitAssumptions: Model compression techniques cannot reduce memory bandwidth requirements sufficiently without unacceptable capability degradation
The gap between cloud and edge inference capability will persist rather than narrow through hardware or algorithmic advances
Consumer willingness to pay for AI features depends on substantive capability rather than marketing positioning

positions.claim: Engram-style architectures that enable O(1) retrieval of pre-stored knowledge will create infrastructure demand across compute, DRAM, and NAND by reducing computational costs while increasing storage requirements
implicitAssumptions: The trade-off between computation and storage favors storage-intensive approaches at current and projected cost structures
Pre-storing knowledge in retrievable form is technically feasible at scale without prohibitive accuracy degradation
Engram-style architectures will be adopted broadly rather than remaining specialized applications

positions.claim: Thermal management innovations like 100% liquid cooling and cabinet-level ecosystems are prerequisite enablers for next-generation high-power AI chips rather than incremental improvements
implicitAssumptions: Power density increases in AI accelerators will continue outpacing air cooling capability improvements
Data center operators will accept the infrastructure complexity and cost of liquid cooling systems to enable performance gains
Thermal constraints are binding limitations on chip design rather than being solvable through architectural changes

positions.claim: The shift from human-generated to model self-generated data accelerates data production and increases reuse value, driving global storage rate increases beyond traditional data growth patterns
implicitAssumptions: Model-generated data has sufficient quality and diversity to be valuable for storage and reuse rather than being ephemeral
The economics of storing and reusing synthetic data are favorable compared to regenerating it on demand
Regulatory or ethical constraints will not significantly limit the storage and reuse of model-generated content
